Scenario.title: J-Magic Campaign: Disruption of Water Infrastructure
Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Simple A low-skill threat group leverages phishing and known vulnerabilities to disrupt critical water infrastructure operations.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Target System Artifact, Network Traffic
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: General Access Method, Exploit TEE Vulnerability, Network Traffic Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker sends spear-phished emails to facility staff, embedding malicious links that redirect to a spoofed login page. Compromised credentials are harvested to establish initial access to the SCADA system.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-67: String Format Overflow in syslog()
stage.description: Using stolen credentials, the attacker exploits an unpatched Juniper router vulnerability (TEE) to execute arbitrary code, gaining control over network devices managing critical infrastructure.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker probes network traffic patterns to identify operational parameters. By manipulating protocol headers, they alter pump speed controls and valve settings, causing service interruptions and potential environmental hazards.

Scenario.summary: A basic-skilled adversary compromises water infrastructure through phishing, exploits unpatched hardware, and manipulates network protocols to induce physical system failures.
